claimText: Dudhwa Tiger Reserve is located in the Terai region of Uttar Pradesh along the Indo-Nepal border.
evidenceExcerpt: Located in the Terai region of Uttar Pradesh along the Indo-Nepal Border.
examUse: Statement-based question on the geographical location of Tiger Reserves.

claimText: Dudhwa Tiger Reserve comprises Dudhwa National Park, Katerniaghat, and Kishanpur.
evidenceExcerpt: Comprises Dudhwa National Park, Katerniaghat, and Kishanpur.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on the constituent protected areas of Tiger Reserves.

claimText: The forest type of Dudhwa Tiger Reserve is characterized by the typical Tarai-Bhabar habitat of the upper Gangetic plains.
evidenceExcerpt: Characterized by typical Tarai-Bhabar habitat of the upper Gangetic plains.
examUse: Statement-based question on the ecological characteristics and forest types of protected areas.
distractorHooks: Semi-arid habitat of the Deccan plateau

claimText: Dudhwa Tiger Reserve is drained by rivers such as Mohana, Suheli, Joraha, and Nagro.
evidenceExcerpt: Drained by rivers such as Mohana, Suheli, Joraha, and Nagro.
examUse: Match-the-following or statement-based question on rivers flowing through National Parks and Tiger Reserves.
distractorHooks: Drained by the Ken and Betwa rivers
